sections.paragraph:
ἀνομέω 1 aor. ἠνόμησα, ptc. ἀνομήσας; pass. inf. ἀνομηθῆναι 3 Km 8:32 (s. ἀνομία; Hdt. 1, 144; pap since III b.c.; LXX; JosAs 12:5; TestLevi 10:3; TestGad 4:1 v.l.; s. Helbing 12; Syn. παρανομέω Suda) be lawless, sin εἴς τινα (Num 32:15; Ps 118:78) against someone Hv 1, 3, 1. Abs. 1 Cl 53:2; B 4:8; 14:3 (all three Ex 32:7). (Ἰωσήφ) ἠνόμησεν σφόδρα sinned exceedingly or flagrantly GJs 15:2.—DELG s.v. 1. νέμω.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

inf. inf. = infinitive

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Helbing Helbing = RH.—List 6

Syn.

Syn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum—List 6

syn. = synopsis, synoptic

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

1 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 85
